Posts a message to the specified thread, making it visible to all thread participants.
This is the primary way agents communicate within an established conversation.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to post to

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The message payload including body and optional metadata

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created message

functions.name: deleteThread
functions.description:
Permanently deletes a mail thread and all its messages.

Removes the thread and its entire message history. This action cannot be undone.
Consider archiving threads instead if you may need to reference them later.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been deleted

functions.name: forceReservation
functions.description:
Force-releases a reservation, overriding the owning agent's hold.

Forcefully frees a resource reservation regardless of the current owner. Use this as
an administrative override when an agent has become unresponsive or the reservation
is stale.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The force-release payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the reservation has been forcefully released

functions.name: getAgent
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific mail agent by its unique identifier.

Returns the agent's registration details including name, capabilities, and
configuration metadata.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the mail agent details

functions.name: getAgentInbox
functions.description:
Retrieves the inbox messages for a specific agent.

Returns all unread and pending messages directed to the specified agent. This is the
primary way an agent checks for new incoming communications.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent whose inbox to fetch

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of inbox messages

functions.name: getAgents
functions.description:
Lists all registered mail agents in the system.

Returns the complete list of agents that are registered to send and receive mail.
Each agent has a unique identity and inbox for receiving messages.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of mail agent registrations

functions.name: getReservations
functions.description:
Lists all currently active resource reservations.

Returns all reservations that have not been released, providing visibility into which
resources are currently claimed by which agents.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering reservations

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of active reservations

functions.name: getThread
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific mail thread by its unique identifier.

Returns the full thread metadata including subject, participants, timestamps, and status.
Use  to fetch the actual messages within the thread.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the mail thread details

functions.name: getThreadMessages
functions.description:
Retrieves all messages within a specific mail thread.

Returns the complete ordered list of messages in a thread, useful for displaying
the full conversation history between agents.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread whose messages to fetch

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of messages in chronological order

functions.name: getThreads
functions.description:
Lists all mail threads with optional filtering and pagination.

Returns conversation threads that group related messages together. Threads can be
filtered by participants, status, or other criteria using the params argument.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering and pagination

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of mail threads

functions.name: markMessageRead
functions.description:
Marks a specific message as read by the current agent.

Updates the read status of a message, which can be used to track which messages
have been processed by each agent in a conversation.

parameters.name: messageId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the message to mark as read

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the read status has been updated

functions.name: releaseReservation
functions.description:
Releases a previously held resource reservation.

Frees the reserved resource so other agents can access it. Agents should release
reservations promptly when they no longer need exclusive access.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The release payload identifying the reservation to free

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the reservation has been released

functions.name: replyToMessage
functions.description:
Sends a reply to an existing message.

Creates a response that is linked to a specific parent message, maintaining the
conversation thread structure. The reply is posted to the same thread as the
original message.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The reply payload including the parent message reference and body

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created reply message

functions.name: searchMessages
functions.description:
Searches messages across all threads using the provided query parameters.

Performs a full-text or filtered search across the mail system, returning messages
that match the specified criteria such as keywords, sender, date range, or thread.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Search query parameters for filtering results

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of matching messages

functions.name: send
functions.description:
Sends a new standalone mail message.

Delivers a message directly without requiring an existing thread. This is useful for
one-off notifications or messages that do not belong to an ongoing conversation.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The message payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the sent message

functions.name: summarizeThread
functions.description:
Generates a summarized view of a mail thread's conversation.

Produces a concise summary of the thread's messages, useful for quickly understanding
the key points of a long conversation without reading every message.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to summarize

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the thread summary

functions.name: updateThread
functions.description:
Partially updates a mail thread's metadata.

Applies a partial update (PATCH) to the thread, allowing you to modify specific fields
such as the subject, status, or participants without affecting other properties.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to patch on the thread

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated mail thread

functions.name: browserNavigate
functions.description:
Navigates to a URL using the browser MCP server.

Sends a navigation command to the browser automation MCP server, instructing it to
load a specific web page. Requires the browser MCP server to be installed and enabled.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The navigation request

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the navigation has been initiated

functions.name: configure
functions.description:
Applies global MCP configuration settings.

Updates the workspace-level MCP settings that affect how all MCP servers are managed,
including default timeout values, transport preferences, and security policies.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The global MCP configuration to apply

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the configuration has been saved

functions.name: configureServer
functions.description:
Configures a specific MCP server's settings.

Updates the connection parameters, environment variables, and other settings for a
named MCP server. The server may need to be restarted for changes to take effect.

parameters.name: serverName
parameters.description: The name of the MCP server to configure

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The server-specific configuration to apply

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the server configuration has been saved

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new custom MCP server configuration.

Registers a custom MCP server that is not part of the standard catalog. This is useful
for connecting to proprietary or locally developed MCP-compatible services.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The custom server creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the server has been created

functions.name: getAll
functions.description:
Retrieves all MCP servers currently registered in the workspace.

Returns every MCP server regardless of its enabled/disabled status, including
connection details, available tools, and current health information.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of all registered MCP servers

functions.name: getAvailableAll
functions.description:
Retrieves all available MCP servers including both marketplace and community servers.

Returns the full unfiltered catalog of MCP servers from all sources. This includes
official, community-contributed, and locally developed servers.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of all available MCP servers

functions.name: getAvailableDetail
functions.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific available MCP server.

Returns comprehensive details about an MCP server from the catalog, including its
description, supported tools, configuration schema, and installation requirements.

parameters.name: mcpId
parameters.description: The identifier of the available MCP server to inspect

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the detailed MCP server information

functions.name: getAvailableList
functions.description:
Retrieves the catalog of MCP servers available for installation.

Returns a curated list of MCP servers that can be installed from the marketplace or
registry. Use this to discover new capabilities to add to the workspace.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of available MCP servers

functions.name: getByName
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific MCP server by its registered name.

Returns the full configuration and status of a single MCP server, including its
tool definitions, resource list, and connection parameters.

parameters.name: serverName
parameters.description: The registered name of the MCP server to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the MCP server details

functions.name: getConfiguredMcps
functions.description:
Retrieves all MCP servers that have been configured in the workspace.

Returns only servers that have been explicitly set up (installed and configured),
as opposed to all available servers in the catalog.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of configured MCP servers

functions.name: getLocalMcpList
functions.description:
Retrieves the list of locally defined MCP servers.

Returns MCP servers that are defined in the local workspace configuration rather than
installed from the marketplace. These are typically custom or development servers.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of local MCP servers

functions.name: getMcpConfigPath
functions.description:
Retrieves the filesystem path to the MCP configuration file.

Returns the absolute path where the MCP configuration is stored on disk. Useful for
manual editing or backup of MCP server configurations.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the MCP configuration file path

functions.name: install
functions.description:
Installs an MCP server from the available catalog.

Downloads and registers an MCP server, making it available for configuration and use.
The server may require additional configuration (e.g., API keys) before it can be started.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The installation request specifying which server to install

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the installation has completed

functions.name: toggle
functions.description:
Toggles an MCP server between enabled and disabled states.

Enables or disables a registered MCP server. Disabled servers will not be started
or connected to, and their tools will not be available to agents.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The toggle request specifying the server and desired state

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the toggle operation has completed

functions.name: updateTools
functions.description:
Updates the tool configurations for MCP servers.

Modifies which tools are exposed or hidden from agents, and updates tool-specific
settings like descriptions, parameter defaults, or access permissions.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The tool update configuration

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the tool configurations have been updated

functions.name: addEpisodicEvents
functions.description:
Appends new events to an episodic memory.

Adds one or more event records to the episodic memory's timeline. Events capture
discrete actions, observations, or state changes that occurred during an operation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The events to add

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the events have been added

functions.name: archiveContextThread
functions.description: Archives a context thread to remove it from active listings.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the context thread to archive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been archived

functions.name: archiveEpisodicMemory
functions.description: Archives an episodic memory to remove it from active listings.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory to archive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the memory has been archived

functions.name: archiveJsonThread
functions.description: Archives a JSON thread to remove it from active listings.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the JSON thread to archive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been archived

functions.name: archiveMarkdownThread
functions.description: Archives a markdown thread to remove it from active listings.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the markdown thread to archive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been archived

functions.name: archiveThread
functions.description:
Archives a generic memory thread to remove it from active listings.

Moves the thread to an archived state where it is preserved but hidden from default
queries. Archived threads can be restored later.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to archive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been archived

functions.name: createContextThread
functions.description:
Creates a new context memory thread.

Initializes a thread designed to capture and store conversational context. Context
threads preserve the state and knowledge gathered during chat interactions for later
recall and summarization.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The context thread creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created context thread

functions.name: createEpisodicMemory
functions.description:
Creates a new episodic memory for tracking temporal events and experiences.

Episodic memories store sequences of events that occurred during agent operations,
similar to how human episodic memory records experiences. They are useful for
learning from past actions and debugging agent behavior.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The episodic memory creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created episodic memory thread

functions.name: createJsonThread
functions.description:
Creates a new JSON-formatted memory thread.

Initializes a thread optimized for storing structured JSON data. JSON threads are
ideal for configuration, structured records, and data that needs programmatic access.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The JSON thread creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created JSON thread

functions.name: createMarkdownThread
functions.description:
Creates a new markdown-formatted memory thread.

Initializes a thread optimized for storing and rendering markdown content. Markdown
threads are ideal for notes, documentation, and free-form text with formatting.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The markdown thread creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created markdown thread

functions.name: createThread
functions.description:
Creates a new generic memory thread.

Initializes a memory thread that can store arbitrary content. Generic threads are the
base type from which specialized thread types (markdown, JSON, context) derive.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The thread creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created memory thread

functions.name: getContextThread
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific context thread by its unique identifier.

Returns the full context thread including captured conversation data and metadata.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the context thread to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the context memory thread

functions.name: getEpisodicAgents
functions.description:
Retrieves the distinct agents that have contributed events to an episodic memory.

Returns the unique set of agent identifiers that recorded events in this memory.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of agent identifier strings

functions.name: getEpisodicEvents
functions.description:
Retrieves events from an episodic memory with optional filtering.

Returns the sequence of events stored in the episodic memory, optionally filtered
by event type, date range, or agent.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional parameters to filter events

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the filtered list of events

functions.name: getEpisodicEventTypes
functions.description:
Retrieves the distinct event types used in an episodic memory.

Returns the unique set of event type strings present in the memory, useful for
building filter UIs or understanding the kinds of events that were recorded.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of event type strings

functions.name: getEpisodicMemory
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific episodic memory by its unique identifier.

Returns the episodic memory metadata. Use  to access
the individual events stored within the memory.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the episodic memory thread

functions.name: getEpisodicTags
functions.description:
Retrieves the distinct tags applied to events in an episodic memory.

Returns the unique set of tag strings used across all events in the memory.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of tag strings

functions.name: getJsonThread
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific JSON thread by its unique identifier.

Returns the full JSON thread including its stored data payload and metadata.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the JSON thread to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the JSON memory thread

functions.name: getMarkdownThread
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific markdown thread by its unique identifier.

Returns the full markdown thread including its rendered content and metadata.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the markdown thread to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the markdown memory thread

functions.name: getThread
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific memory thread by its unique identifier.

Returns the full thread object including metadata, content references, and status.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the memory thread details

functions.name: listContextThreads
functions.description:
Lists all context memory threads with optional filtering.

Returns only threads of the context type, filtered by the provided parameters.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional filtering and pagination parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of context memory threads

functions.name: listEpisodicMemories
functions.description:
Lists all episodic memories with optional filtering.

Returns episodic memory records that match the provided criteria.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional filtering and pagination parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of episodic memory threads

functions.name: listJsonThreads
functions.description:
Lists all JSON memory threads with optional filtering.

Returns only threads of the JSON type, filtered by the provided parameters.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional filtering and pagination parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of JSON memory threads

functions.name: listMarkdownThreads
functions.description:
Lists all markdown memory threads with optional filtering.

Returns only threads of the markdown type, filtered by the provided parameters.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional filtering and pagination parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of markdown memory threads

functions.name: listThreads
functions.description:
Lists all generic memory threads with optional filtering.

Returns memory threads that match the provided filter criteria. Use this to browse
or search through stored memory threads.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional filtering and pagination parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of memory threads

functions.name: saveContextFromChat
functions.description:
Saves context extracted from a chat conversation into a context thread.

Captures relevant information, decisions, and knowledge from an ongoing or completed
chat session and persists it in the specified context thread for future reference.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the context thread to save into

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The chat context extraction payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the context has been saved

functions.name: summarizeContextThread
functions.description:
Generates a summary of the context stored in a thread.

Uses LLM processing to create a concise summary of all the context captured in the
thread. Useful for creating condensed knowledge bases from lengthy conversations.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the context thread to summarize

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional summarization parameters (e.g., max length, focus topics)

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the generated summary

functions.name: unarchiveEpisodicMemory
functions.description: Restores an archived episodic memory back to active status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory to unarchive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the memory has been unarchived

functions.name: unarchiveJsonThread
functions.description: Restores an archived JSON thread back to active status.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the JSON thread to unarchive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been unarchived

functions.name: unarchiveMarkdownThread
functions.description: Restores an archived markdown thread back to active status.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the markdown thread to unarchive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been unarchived

functions.name: updateContextThread
functions.description:
Updates the metadata of a context thread.

Modifies thread-level properties such as name and tags without affecting the stored
conversational context.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the context thread to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The metadata fields to update

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated context thread

functions.name: updateEpisodicMemory
functions.description: Updates the metadata of an episodic memory.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the episodic memory to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated episodic memory thread

functions.name: updateJsonThread
functions.description:
Updates the metadata of a JSON thread (name, tags, etc.).

Modifies thread-level properties without changing the stored JSON data.
Use  to change the actual data payload.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the JSON thread to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The metadata fields to update

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated JSON thread

functions.name: updateJsonThreadData
functions.description:
Replaces the JSON data payload of a thread.

Updates the structured data stored in the JSON thread. This is separate from metadata
updates to allow efficient data-only writes.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the JSON thread

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The new JSON data to store

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the data has been updated

functions.name: updateMarkdownThread
functions.description:
Updates the metadata of a markdown thread (name, tags, etc.).

Modifies thread-level properties without changing the markdown content itself.
Use  to change the actual markdown text.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the markdown thread to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The metadata fields to update

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated markdown thread

functions.name: updateMarkdownThreadContent
functions.description:
Replaces the markdown content of a thread.

Updates the actual markdown text content stored in the thread. This is separate from
metadata updates to allow efficient content-only writes.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the markdown thread

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The new markdown content

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the content has been updated

functions.name: updateThread
functions.description:
Updates the metadata or content of a generic memory thread.

Modifies an existing thread's properties such as name, tags, or stored data.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the thread

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated memory thread

functions.name: activatePipeline
functions.description:
Activates a disabled ingestion pipeline.

Enables the pipeline so it can be triggered by events or manual execution. A pipeline
must be activated before it will process any data.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline to activate

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional activation parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the pipeline has been activated

functions.name: createPipeline
functions.description:
Creates a new ingestion pipeline with the specified processor chain.

Defines a new data processing workflow that can be triggered manually or automatically
in response to events. The pipeline chains processors together to transform data
before storing it in memory.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The pipeline creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created pipeline

functions.name: deletePipeline
functions.description:
Permanently deletes an ingestion pipeline.

Removes the pipeline configuration. Previously ingested data is not affected.
The pipeline must be disabled before deletion.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the pipeline has been deleted

functions.name: disablePipeline
functions.description:
Disables an active ingestion pipeline.

Prevents the pipeline from being triggered or executed until it is reactivated.
Any in-progress executions may continue to completion.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline to disable

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional deactivation parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the pipeline has been disabled

functions.name: duplicatePipeline
functions.description:
Creates a copy of an existing ingestion pipeline.

Duplicates the pipeline configuration, optionally with modifications. Useful for
creating variations of working pipelines without starting from scratch.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline to duplicate

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional modifications to apply to the duplicated pipeline

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created duplicate pipeline

functions.name: executePipeline
functions.description:
Manually triggers execution of an ingestion pipeline.

Runs the pipeline's processor chain against the provided input data or its configured
data source. The execution is asynchronous; monitor progress through events.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline to execute

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional execution parameters and input data

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the execution has been initiated

functions.name: getEventSchema
functions.description:
Retrieves the schema for a specific ingestion event type.

Returns the field definitions and validation rules for a single event type,
useful for building event handlers or display components.

parameters.name: eventType
parameters.description: The event type identifier to get the schema for

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the event schema definition

functions.name: getEventSchemas
functions.description:
Retrieves all event schemas that define the structure of ingestion events.

Returns the schema definitions for each event type emitted by the ingestion system,
describing the fields and data types contained in each event.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of event schema definitions

functions.name: getPipeline
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific ingestion pipeline by its unique identifier.

Returns the full pipeline configuration including its processor chain, trigger
conditions, and execution history.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the ingestion pipeline details

functions.name: getProcessors
functions.description:
Retrieves all available processor types that can be used in pipelines.

Returns the catalog of processors (text splitters, embedders, classifiers, etc.)
that can be chained together in ingestion pipelines.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of available processor definitions

functions.name: listEvents
functions.description:
Lists ingestion events with optional filtering.

Returns records of ingestion pipeline executions, including success/failure status,
processing metrics, and error details. Useful for monitoring and debugging pipelines.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering events

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of ingestion events

functions.name: listPipelines
functions.description:
Lists all ingestion pipelines with optional filtering.

Returns the collection of configured ingestion pipelines. Each pipeline defines a
sequence of processing steps for transforming raw data into memory entries.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering and pagination

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of ingestion pipelines

functions.name: updatePipeline
functions.description:
Updates an existing ingestion pipeline's configuration.

Modifies the pipeline's processor chain, triggers, or metadata. Changes affect
subsequent executions but do not alter previously ingested data.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the pipeline

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated pipeline

functions.name: validate
functions.description:
Validates an ingestion pipeline configuration without creating or executing it.

Checks that the pipeline definition is well-formed, all referenced processors exist,
and the processor chain is compatible. Use this to catch errors before saving.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The pipeline configuration to validate

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the validation result

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new orchestrator instance with the specified configuration.

Sets up a new orchestrator that will coordinate agent interactions according to the
provided rules and workflow definitions. The orchestrator can be started immediately
or configured further before activation.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The orchestrator creation configuration

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created orchestrator instance

functions.name: delete
functions.description:
Deletes an orchestrator instance permanently.

Removes the orchestrator and its configuration. Any active workflows managed by this
orchestrator should be stopped before deletion.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the orchestrator to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the orchestrator has been deleted

functions.name: get
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific orchestrator instance by its unique identifier.

Returns the full configuration and current state of an orchestrator, including its
assigned agents, coordination rules, and execution history.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the orchestrator to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the orchestrator instance details

functions.name: list
functions.description:
Lists all orchestrator instances with optional filtering.

Returns the collection of configured orchestrators, which can be filtered by status,
type, or other criteria. Each orchestrator defines a coordination strategy for
multi-agent workflows.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional filtering and pagination parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of orchestrator instances

functions.name: update
functions.description:
Updates an existing orchestrator instance's configuration.

Modifies the settings, rules, or agent assignments of an orchestrator. Changes take
effect for subsequent workflow executions.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the orchestrator to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the orchestrator

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated orchestrator instance

functions.name: createType
functions.description:
Creates a new persistent memory type with a defined processing pipeline.

Registers a new memory type that describes how input data should be processed and
stored. The type definition includes a sequence of steps that will be executed
when the pipeline runs.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The type creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created persistent memory type

functions.name: deleteType
functions.description:
Permanently deletes a persistent memory type definition.

Removes the type and its pipeline configuration. Previously generated memory entries
are not affected by the deletion.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the type to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the type has been deleted

functions.name: duplicateType
functions.description:
Creates a copy of an existing persistent memory type.

Duplicates the type definition, optionally with modifications such as a new name.
Useful for creating variations of working pipelines.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the type to duplicate

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional modifications to apply to the duplicated type

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created duplicate type

functions.name: executeType
functions.description:
Executes a persistent memory type's processing pipeline.

Runs the defined processing steps against the provided input data or configured
data source, generating new memory entries as output.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the type to execute

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional execution parameters and input data

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the execution has been initiated

functions.name: exportType
functions.description:
Exports a persistent memory type configuration as a portable format.

Serializes the type definition for sharing, backup, or migration to another workspace.
The exported data can be imported using .

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the type to export

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the exported type configuration

functions.name: getStepSpecs
functions.description:
Retrieves all available processing step specifications.

Returns the catalog of step types that can be used when building persistent memory
pipelines. Each spec describes a step's inputs, outputs, and configurable parameters.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of step specifications

functions.name: getType
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific persistent memory type by its unique identifier.

Returns the full type definition including its processing steps, configuration,
and metadata.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the persistent memory type

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the persistent memory type definition

functions.name: getUserConfigurableStepSpecs
functions.description:
Retrieves step specifications that are user-configurable.

Returns a subset of step specifications that expose user-facing configuration options,
filtering out internal-only or system-managed steps.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of user-configurable step specifications

functions.name: importType
functions.description:
Imports a persistent memory type configuration from an exported format.

Creates a new persistent memory type from previously exported data. This enables
sharing pipeline configurations between workspaces or restoring from backups.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The import payload containing the exported type configuration

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly imported persistent memory type

functions.name: listTypes
functions.description:
Lists all persistent memory type definitions with optional filtering.

Returns the collection of defined persistent memory types, each describing a reusable
processing pipeline for generating memory entries.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering and pagination

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of persistent memory types

functions.name: updateType
functions.description:
Updates an existing persistent memory type's configuration.

Modifies the type's processing steps, name, or other settings. Changes affect
subsequent executions but do not alter previously generated memories.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the type to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated persistent memory type

functions.name: validate
functions.description:
Validates a persistent memory type configuration without saving it.

Checks that the type definition is well-formed, all referenced step types exist,
and the processing chain is compatible. Use this before creating or updating a type
to catch configuration errors early.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The type configuration to validate

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the validation result

functions.name: createTask
functions.description:
Creates a new task in the planner.

Adds a task with a title, description, and optional agent assignment. Tasks can have
dependencies, priorities, and labels to organize complex project work.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The task creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created planner task

functions.name: createTasksFromMarkdown
functions.description:
Bulk-creates tasks by parsing a markdown document.

Parses a markdown-formatted task list (using checkboxes, headings, and lists) and
creates individual planner tasks from it. This enables rapid task creation from
planning documents or specifications.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The request containing the markdown content to parse

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of the newly created tasks

functions.name: deleteTask
functions.description:
Deletes a planner task permanently.

Removes a task from the planner. This action cannot be undone. Consider updating the
task status to "cancelled" instead if you want to preserve a record of the work.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the task has been deleted

functions.name: exportMarkdown
functions.description:
Exports the current planner tasks as a markdown document.

Generates a markdown-formatted representation of all tasks, organized by agent or
status. Useful for creating reports, sharing progress externally, or backing up
the current plan.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional parameters to control which tasks are exported and formatting

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the markdown content

functions.name: get
functions.description:
Retrieves the planner overview with aggregated status information.

Returns a high-level summary of the planner state including total tasks, completion
statistics, and agent utilization. Use this for dashboard views and progress monitoring.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the planner overview data

functions.name: getAgents
functions.description:
Retrieves all agents registered in the planner.

Returns the list of agents that have been assigned tasks or are available for task
assignment. Each agent includes its current workload and assignment information.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of planner agents

functions.name: getAgentTasks
functions.description:
Retrieves all tasks assigned to a specific agent.

Returns the task list for a given agent, allowing you to see what work is queued,
in progress, or completed for that agent.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent whose tasks to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of tasks assigned to the agent

functions.name: updateTask
functions.description:
Updates an existing planner task.

Modifies task properties such as status, assignment, priority, or description.
Use this to transition tasks through their lifecycle (e.g., pending to in-progress
to completed).

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the task

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated planner task

functions.name: getDefaultProfile
functions.description:
Retrieves the default user profile for the current session.

Returns the full profile object including user identity, preferences, and configured
defaults. Use this to display user information or check profile settings.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the user's profile data

functions.name: setDefaultAgent
functions.description:
Sets the default agent for the user's profile.

Configures which agent is automatically selected when the user starts a new
conversation or task. This persists across sessions until changed.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The request containing the agent to set as default

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the default agent has been updated

functions.name: assignThreadToGroup
functions.description:
Assigns a conversation thread to a task group.

Links a thread to a task group for organizational purposes. A thread can belong
to at most one group at a time.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The assignment request

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the assignment has been saved

functions.name: checkEnvironment
functions.description:
Checks the availability and configuration of an environment variable or dependency.

Validates that required environment variables, tools, or runtime dependencies are
properly configured for the project.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The environment check request

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the check result

functions.name: checkProjectName
functions.description:
Checks whether a project name is available for use.

Validates that the proposed project name does not conflict with existing projects
in the workspace. Use this before creating a new project.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The name check request

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an object indicating availability

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new project in a specified workspace.

Initializes a new project with the given configuration. The project will be created
in the specified workspace directory with its own configuration and history.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The project creation payload including name and workspace details

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created project

functions.name: createInDefault
functions.description:
Creates a new project in the default workspace.

Convenience method that creates a project without needing to specify a workspace.
The project is placed in the system's default workspace directory.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The project creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created project

functions.name: createTaskGroup
functions.description:
Creates a new task group for organizing conversation threads.

Defines a new group that threads can be assigned to for organizational purposes.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The task group creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created task group

functions.name: deleteTaskGroup
functions.description:
Permanently deletes a task group.

Removes the task group. Threads previously assigned to this group will become
unassigned but are not deleted.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task group to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the task group has been deleted

functions.name: getAllWithUniqueId
functions.description:
Retrieves all projects with their unique identifiers.

Returns the complete list of projects matching the request criteria, each with a
guaranteed unique ID for cross-referencing.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The request specifying which projects to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of projects with unique IDs

functions.name: getByWorkspace
functions.description:
Retrieves all projects belonging to a specific workspace.

Returns the list of projects within the specified workspace, useful for workspace
navigation and project switching.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the workspace

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of projects in the workspace

functions.name: getChatHistory
functions.description:
Retrieves the chat history for the current project.

Returns an array of past chat sessions and their messages, providing a record of
all conversations that have occurred within the project context.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of chat history entries

functions.name: getConfigs
functions.description:
Retrieves the configuration settings for the current project.

Returns project-level settings including build commands, environment variables,
agent preferences, and other configurable options.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the project configuration

functions.name: getDebugHistory
functions.description:
Retrieves the debug session history for the current project.

Returns records of past debugging sessions including breakpoints hit, variable
states, and step-through sequences.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of debug history entries

functions.name: getDebugIndex
functions.description:
Retrieves the debug index for the current project.

Returns the indexed debug information that enables fast lookup of debug sessions,
breakpoint locations, and diagnostic data.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the debug index data

functions.name: getRoot
functions.description:
Retrieves the root project information for the current workspace.

Returns the top-level project metadata including name, path, status, and configuration
references. This is typically the entry point for inspecting the active project.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the root project details

functions.name: getTaskGroups
functions.description:
Retrieves all task groups defined for the current project.

Task groups organize conversation threads into logical categories for better
workflow management and navigation.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of task groups

functions.name: getThreadGroup
functions.description:
Retrieves the task group that a specific thread is assigned to.

Returns the group assignment for a thread, or null if the thread is not assigned
to any group.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The identifier of the thread to look up

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the assigned task group, or null if unassigned

functions.name: getThreadGroupMappings
functions.description:
Retrieves all thread-to-group assignment mappings.

Returns the complete mapping of which threads are assigned to which task groups,
useful for building navigation or overview UIs.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of thread-group mapping entries

functions.name: getThreads
functions.description:
Retrieves all conversation threads associated with the current project.

Returns the list of active and archived threads, which represent individual
conversation streams within the project.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of project threads

functions.name: getThreadsForGroup
functions.description:
Retrieves all thread identifiers assigned to a specific task group.

Returns the list of thread IDs that belong to the specified group.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task group

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of thread identifier strings

functions.name: getTreeBatch
functions.description:
Retrieves multiple tree items in a single batch request.

Fetches details for several tree items at once, reducing round trips compared to
individual  calls. Useful when loading multiple files or directories.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The batch request containing the item IDs to fetch

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of tree items

functions.name: getTreeChildren
functions.description:
Retrieves the child items of a specific node in the project file tree.

Returns the immediate children (files and subdirectories) of the specified parent
directory in the project tree. Use this for lazy-loading tree navigation.

parameters.name: parentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent tree node

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of child tree items

functions.name: getTreeItem
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific item from the project's file tree by its identifier.

Returns metadata about a file or directory in the project tree, including its name,
path, type, and any associated metadata.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the tree item to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the tree item details

functions.name: resetProject
functions.description:
Resets a project to its initial state.

Clears the project's chat history, debug sessions, and configuration changes,
restoring it to a clean state. This is a destructive operation that cannot be undone.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The reset request identifying the project and reset scope

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the project has been reset

functions.name: runCommand
functions.description:
Executes a shell command within the project's working directory.

Runs a command (e.g., build, test, lint) in the context of the project, with access
to the project's environment variables and path configuration.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The command execution request

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the command execution result

functions.name: setActive
functions.description:
Sets a project as the currently active project in the workspace.

Switches the active project context, which affects which project's files, configuration,
and history are used for subsequent operations.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The request identifying which project to activate

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the active project has been switched

functions.name: updateCodeboltYaml
functions.description:
Updates the codebolt.yaml configuration file for the project.

Modifies the project's codebolt.yaml, which defines agent configurations, tool
permissions, and workflow settings specific to the CodeBolt platform.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The YAML configuration updates to apply

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the YAML has been updated

functions.name: updateConfig
functions.description:
Updates the project configuration via an alternative endpoint.

Applies configuration changes to the project settings. Functions similarly to
 but uses a different server route.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The configuration fields to update

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the configuration has been saved

functions.name: updateProjectConfigs
functions.description:
Updates the project configuration settings.

Applies changes to the project-level configuration. This is the primary way to modify
project settings like build commands, environment variables, and agent defaults.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The configuration fields to update

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the configuration has been saved

functions.name: updateTaskGroup
functions.description:
Updates an existing task group's properties.

Modifies the task group's name, description, or other metadata.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task group to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the task group

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated task group

functions.name: addItem
functions.description:
Adds an item to a specific section within a package.

Registers a new item (e.g., a file, class, or endpoint reference) under the specified
section of a package, enriching the structural metadata.

parameters.name: packageId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the package

parameters.name: section
parameters.description: The name of the section to add the item to (e.g., 'routes', 'models')

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The item definition to add

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the item has been added

functions.name: createPackage
functions.description:
Creates a new package definition in the project structure.

Adds a logical package to the workspace structure with the given name and configuration.
Packages group related code sections and items together.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The package creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created package definition

functions.name: deleteItem
functions.description:
Removes an item from a package section.

Deletes the structural reference to an item within a package section. This only removes
the metadata entry, not the actual underlying file or resource.

parameters.name: packageId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the package

parameters.name: section
parameters.description: The name of the section containing the item

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the item to remove

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the item has been removed

functions.name: deletePackage
functions.description:
Deletes a package and all its sections and items from the project structure.

Permanently removes the package definition. This does not delete actual source files,
only the structural metadata describing the package.

parameters.name: packageId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the package to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the package has been deleted

functions.name: getMetadata
functions.description:
Retrieves the full workspace metadata including package definitions and structure.

Returns the complete structural representation of the workspace, which describes how
the project is organized into packages, modules, and sections.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the workspace metadata

functions.name: getPackage
functions.description:
Retrieves a single package by its unique identifier.

Returns the full package definition including all its sections and items.

parameters.name: packageId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the package to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the package definition

functions.name: getPackages
functions.description:
Retrieves all package definitions in the workspace.

Returns the list of logical packages that make up the project, each containing
its sections, items, and metadata.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of package definitions

functions.name: saveMetadata

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The complete workspace metadata to save

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the metadata has been saved

functions.name: updatePackage
functions.description:
Updates an existing package definition.

Modifies the metadata and configuration of a package. Does not affect the package's
sections or items; use  and  for those operations.

parameters.name: packageId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the package to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the package

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated package definition

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new requirement plan.

Registers a new requirement plan with the system using the provided
specification. The plan can include project goals, feature lists,
acceptance criteria, and other requirement documentation.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Request containing the plan details

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created RequirementPlan object

functions.name: ensureFolder
functions.description:
Ensures the requirement plan folder exists in the workspace.

Creates the requirement plan directory if it doesn't already exist,
establishing a location for storing plan files and related documents.
This operation is idempotent and safe to call multiple times.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional parameters for folder creation

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the folder is ensured

functions.name: get
functions.description:
Retrieves a requirement plan based on query parameters.

Returns the plan matching the specified criteria, typically by project
ID or plan name. If multiple plans match, the most relevant one is returned.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Query parameters to identify the plan

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the RequirementPlan object

functions.name: update
functions.description:
Updates an existing requirement plan.

Modifies the content, status, or structure of a requirement plan.
Use this to add new requirements, update priorities, change status,
or make other modifications as the project evolves.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Request containing the plan updates

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated RequirementPlan object

functions.name: addLinkedJob
functions.description:
Links an existing job to a review-merge request.

Associates a job with this review so that changes from the job are tracked as part
of the review workflow.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The linked job details including the job ID to associate

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the job has been linked

functions.name: addPheromone
functions.description:
Adds a pheromone tag to a review-merge request.

Pheromones are metadata signals used for inter-agent communication. Adding a pheromone
allows agents to tag reviews with contextual information that other agents can detect.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The pheromone data to attach

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the pheromone has been added

functions.name: addProposedJob
functions.description:
Adds a proposed job to a review-merge request.

Proposed jobs are suggested work items that arise from the review process. They must
be approved before being converted into actual jobs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The proposed job details

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the proposed job has been added

functions.name: addReview
functions.description:
Adds a review or feedback to a review-merge request.

Submits review feedback (approval, rejection, or comments) from a reviewer.
Multiple reviews can be added from different reviewers.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The review feedback payload containing the reviewer's assessment

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the review has been added

functions.name: approveProposedJob
functions.description:
Approves a proposed job on a review-merge request.

Marks the proposed job as approved, making it eligible for conversion into an actual job.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: proposedJobId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the proposed job to approve

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional additional data for the approval

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the proposed job has been approved

functions.name: approveUnlockRequest
functions.description:
Approves an unlock request, releasing the lock on a review-merge request.

The lock holder calls this to grant an unlock request from another agent.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: unlockRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the unlock request to approve

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional additional data for the approval

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the unlock request has been approved

functions.name: convertProposedJob
functions.description:
Converts an approved proposed job into an actual job.

Takes a previously approved proposed job and creates a real job from it. The proposed
job must have been approved before conversion.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: proposedJobId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the approved proposed job to convert

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional additional data for the conversion

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the proposed job has been converted

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new review-merge request.

Initiates a review workflow for a set of changes. The request will enter the pending
state and can be reviewed, locked, and eventually merged.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The review-merge request creation payload

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created ReviewMergeRequest

functions.name: createUnlockRequest
functions.description:
Creates an unlock request for a locked review-merge request.

When a review is locked by another agent, this creates a formal request to unlock it.
The lock holder can then approve or reject the unlock request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the locked review-merge request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The unlock request payload with reason and requester information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the unlock request has been created

functions.name: delete
functions.description:
Deletes a review-merge request.

Permanently removes a review-merge request and all associated data. This action
cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the deletion is complete

functions.name: get
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific review-merge request by its ID.

Fetches the full details of a single review-merge request including its current
status, reviews, pheromones, and linked jobs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the ReviewMergeRequest object

functions.name: getAll
functions.description:
Retrieves all review-merge requests, optionally filtered by query parameters.

Returns the complete list of review-merge requests. Use the optional params to filter
by status, date range, or other criteria supported by the server.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering the results

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of ReviewMergeRequest objects

functions.name: getByAgent
functions.description:
Retrieves all review-merge requests associated with a specific agent.

Filters requests by the agent that created or is assigned to them. Useful for
building agent-specific review dashboards or tracking an agent's review workload.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent to filter by

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of ReviewMergeRequest objects for the agent

functions.name: getBySwarm
functions.description:
Retrieves all review-merge requests associated with a specific swarm.

Filters requests by the swarm that generated them. Useful for monitoring review
activity within a particular swarm context.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm to filter by

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of ReviewMergeRequest objects for the swarm

functions.name: getPending
functions.description:
Retrieves all pending review-merge requests.

Returns requests that are awaiting review and have not yet been approved or rejected.
Use this to build a review queue or dashboard showing outstanding work.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of pending ReviewMergeRequest objects

functions.name: getPheromoneTypes
functions.description:
Retrieves the list of available pheromone types.

Pheromones are tags or signals that can be attached to review requests to communicate
metadata to other agents. This returns the set of recognized pheromone type strings.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of pheromone type name strings

functions.name: getReadyToMerge
functions.description:
Retrieves all review-merge requests that are approved and ready to merge.

Filters for requests that have passed all review criteria and can be merged immediately.
Useful for automated merge pipelines or merge-ready dashboards.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of merge-ready ReviewMergeRequest objects

functions.name: getStatistics
functions.description:
Retrieves aggregate statistics about review-merge requests.

Returns counts and metrics across all review states, useful for monitoring review
throughput and identifying bottlenecks in the review process.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to a ReviewMergeStatistics object with aggregate data

functions.name: lock
functions.description:
Locks a review-merge request to prevent concurrent modifications.

Acquiring a lock ensures exclusive access while performing operations on the request.
Other agents attempting to modify a locked request will be blocked.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request to lock

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional lock configuration parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the lock has been acquired

functions.name: merge
functions.description:
Merges an approved review-merge request.

Executes the final merge operation on a review that has been approved and is ready
to merge. This applies the associated changes to the target branch or codebase.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request to merge

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional merge configuration parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the merge is complete

functions.name: rejectProposedJob
functions.description:
Rejects a proposed job on a review-merge request.

Marks the proposed job as rejected, preventing it from being converted into an actual job.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: proposedJobId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the proposed job to reject

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional additional data such as rejection reason

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the proposed job has been rejected

functions.name: rejectUnlockRequest
functions.description:
Rejects an unlock request, keeping the review-merge request locked.

The lock holder calls this to deny an unlock request from another agent.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: unlockRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the unlock request to reject

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional additional data such as rejection reason

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the unlock request has been rejected

functions.name: removeLinkedJob
functions.description:
Removes a linked job from a review-merge request.

Disassociates a previously linked job from the review request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the job to unlink

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the job has been unlinked

functions.name: removePheromone
functions.description:
Removes a pheromone tag from a review-merge request.

Detaches a previously added pheromone by its type string from the review request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The pheromone type string to remove

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the pheromone has been removed

functions.name: unlock
functions.description:
Unlocks a previously locked review-merge request.

Releases the lock on a review request, allowing other agents to modify it again.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request to unlock

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional unlock parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the lock has been released

functions.name: update
functions.description:
Updates an existing review-merge request.

Modifies the mutable fields of a review-merge request such as title, description,
or other metadata. Does not change the review status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated ReviewMergeRequest

functions.name: updateStatus
functions.description:
Updates the status of a review-merge request.

Transitions the request to a new review status (e.g., approved, rejected, needs-changes).
This is the primary mechanism for progressing a request through the review workflow.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the review-merge request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The status update payload containing the new status

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the status has been updated

functions.name: createFeature
functions.description:
Creates a new feature within a specific phase.

Adds a feature to the roadmap under the specified phase. Features represent
discrete pieces of functionality planned for delivery.

parameters.name: phaseId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the phase to add the feature to

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The feature creation payload including title and description

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created RoadmapFeature

functions.name: createIdea
functions.description:
Creates a new idea in the roadmap idea pool.

Adds an idea that can later be reviewed, refined, and potentially promoted
to a full feature on the roadmap.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The idea creation payload including title and description

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created RoadmapIdea

functions.name: createPhase
functions.description:
Creates a new roadmap phase.

Adds a high-level phase to the roadmap for organizing features. Phases typically
represent milestones, sprints, or release versions.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The phase creation payload including name and optional description

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created RoadmapPhase

functions.name: createTaskFromFeature
functions.description:
Creates a task from a roadmap feature.

Converts a feature into an actionable task in the task management system. This
bridges the gap between roadmap planning and task execution.

parameters.name: featureId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the feature to create a task from

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional task creation parameters to override defaults

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the task has been created

functions.name: deleteFeature
functions.description:
Deletes a feature from the roadmap.

Permanently removes a feature. This action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: featureId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the feature to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the feature has been deleted

functions.name: deleteIdea
functions.description:
Deletes an idea from the roadmap idea pool.

Permanently removes an idea. This action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: ideaId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the idea to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the idea has been deleted

functions.name: deletePhase
functions.description:
Deletes a roadmap phase.
