Permanently removes a phase and potentially its associated features. This action
cannot be undone.

parameters.name: phaseId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the phase to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the phase has been deleted

functions.name: get
functions.description:
Retrieves the full roadmap with all phases.

Returns the complete roadmap structure including all phases and their nested features.
Use optional query parameters to filter or paginate results.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering the roadmap

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of RoadmapPhase objects

functions.name: getFeature
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific feature by its ID.

Fetches the full details of a single feature including its status, description,
and associated metadata.

parameters.name: featureId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the feature

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the RoadmapFeature object

functions.name: getFeatures
functions.description:
Retrieves all features across all roadmap phases.

Returns a flat list of all features regardless of which phase they belong to.
Use optional query parameters to filter by status, priority, or other criteria.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering features

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of RoadmapFeature objects

functions.name: getIdea
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific idea by its ID.

Fetches the full details of a single idea including its status and review history.

parameters.name: ideaId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the idea

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the RoadmapIdea object

functions.name: getIdeas
functions.description:
Retrieves all ideas in the roadmap idea pool.

Ideas are proposals that have not yet been promoted to features. Use optional
query parameters to filter by status or other criteria.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering ideas

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of RoadmapIdea objects

functions.name: getPhase
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific roadmap phase by its ID.

Fetches the full details of a single phase including its metadata and associated features.

parameters.name: phaseId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the phase

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the RoadmapPhase object

functions.name: moveFeature
functions.description:
Moves a feature to a different phase or reorders it within its current phase.

Use this to reorganize the roadmap by relocating features between phases or
changing the display order of features within a phase.

parameters.name: featureId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the feature to move

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The move parameters including target phase and/or position

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the feature has been moved

functions.name: moveIdeaToRoadmap
functions.description:
Promotes an idea to the roadmap as a feature.

Converts a reviewed and approved idea into a feature within a specified roadmap phase.
This is the final step in the idea-to-feature pipeline.

parameters.name: ideaId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the idea to promote

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional parameters including target phase for the new feature

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the idea has been promoted to a feature

functions.name: reviewIdea
functions.description:
Submits an idea for review.

Moves an idea into the review process where it can be evaluated for potential
promotion to a roadmap feature.

parameters.name: ideaId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the idea to review

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional review parameters such as reviewer assignment

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the idea has been submitted for review

functions.name: sendFeatureToChat
functions.description:
Sends a feature to the chat for discussion.

Shares the feature details in a chat context so that team members or agents can
discuss and collaborate on the feature requirements.

parameters.name: featureId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the feature to send to chat

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional parameters controlling how the feature is shared

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the feature has been sent to chat

functions.name: updateFeature
functions.description:
Updates an existing feature.

Modifies the properties of a feature such as its title, description, status, or priority.

parameters.name: featureId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the feature to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the feature

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated RoadmapFeature

functions.name: updateIdea
functions.description:
Updates an existing idea.

Modifies the properties of an idea such as its title, description, or status.

parameters.name: ideaId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the idea to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the idea

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated RoadmapIdea

functions.name: updatePhase
functions.description:
Updates an existing roadmap phase.

Modifies the properties of a phase such as its name, description, or ordering.

parameters.name: phaseId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the phase to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The fields to update on the phase

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated RoadmapPhase

functions.name: checkpoint
functions.description: Create a shadow git checkpoint

functions.name: cleanup
functions.description: Clean up shadow git data

functions.name: diff
functions.description: Get diff between shadow git checkpoints

functions.name: getCheckpoints
functions.description: List all shadow git checkpoints

functions.name: initialize
functions.description: Initialize shadow git tracking

functions.name: readFile
functions.description: Read a file from a shadow git checkpoint

functions.name: restore
functions.description: Restore to a previous shadow git checkpoint

modules.name: sockets
modules.description: Socket namespace providing lazy access to all WebSocket connections

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new specification document.

Registers a new spec with the system using the provided content.
The spec can include technical details, API definitions, architecture
descriptions, and other structured specification information.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Request containing the spec details

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created Spec object

functions.name: ensureFolder
functions.description:
Ensures the specs folder exists in the workspace.

Creates the specification directory if it doesn't already exist,
establishing a location for storing spec files and related documentation.
This operation is idempotent and safe to call multiple times.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional parameters for folder creation

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the folder is ensured

functions.name: get
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific specification document.

Returns the spec matching the provided query parameters, typically
by spec ID or project. The document includes all sections, metadata,
and version information.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Query parameters to identify the spec

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the Spec object

functions.name: list
functions.description:
Lists all specification documents.

Returns every spec document in the system, optionally filtered by
project, type, or other criteria. Use this for browsing specs or
populating specification management UIs.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering specs

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of Spec objects

functions.name: update
functions.description:
Updates an existing specification document.

Modifies the content, metadata, or structure of a spec. Use this to
add new sections, update information, change versions, or make other
modifications as specifications evolve.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Request containing the spec updates

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated Spec object

functions.name: addAgent
functions.description:
Add an agent to a swarm.

Registers a new agent with the swarm, making it available for assignment
to teams and roles. Agents are the worker entities that perform tasks
within the swarm context.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The agent registration information

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the registered SwarmAgentInfo object

functions.name: applyToVacancy
functions.description:
Apply to a vacancy in a swarm.

Submits an application from an agent for a specific vacancy. The agent
will be considered for the position and may be selected based on
qualifications and availability.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: vacancyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the vacancy

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional application information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the application is submitted

functions.name: approveSpawnRequest
functions.description:
Approve a spawn request.

Approves a pending spawn request, authorizing the creation of new
agent instances. The agents will be spawned according to the
request specifications.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the spawn request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional approval information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the request is approved

functions.name: approveTerminationRequest
functions.description:
Approve a termination request.

Approves a pending termination request, authorizing the removal
of specified agent instances. The agents will be terminated
gracefully.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the termination request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional approval information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the request is approved

functions.name: assignRole
functions.description:
Assign a role to an agent in a swarm.

Grants a role to an agent, giving them the associated permissions
and responsibilities. The agent can now perform actions authorized
by this role.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the role to assign

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The role assignment information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the role is assigned

functions.name: closeVacancy
functions.description:
Close a vacancy in a swarm.

Marks a vacancy as closed, preventing further applications. This is
used when a position has been filled or is no longer available.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: vacancyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the vacancy to close

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional closure information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the vacancy is closed

functions.name: createRole
functions.description:
Create a role in a swarm.

Creates a new role that agents can be assigned to. Roles define
agent responsibilities, permissions, and capabilities within the
swarm context.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The role creation information

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created SwarmRole object

functions.name: createSpawnRequest
functions.description:
Create a spawn request for a swarm.

Submits a request to spawn new agent instances within the swarm.
This is used to dynamically scale the swarm by adding more agents
to handle increased workload.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The spawn request details

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created SpawnRequest object

functions.name: createSwarm
functions.description:
Create a new swarm.

Initializes a new swarm with the specified configuration. Swarms
are the top-level organizational unit for multi-agent coordination,
containing teams, roles, and agents that work together on tasks.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The swarm configuration and metadata

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created SwarmData object

functions.name: createTeam
functions.description:
Create a team in a swarm.

Creates a new team within the swarm, providing organizational structure
for grouping agents. Teams can be assigned specific goals and tasks,
with agents collaborating within the team context.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The team creation information

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created SwarmTeam object

functions.name: createTerminationRequest
functions.description:
Create a termination request for a swarm.

Submits a request to terminate specific agent instances within
the swarm. This is used to reduce swarm size or remove specific
agents.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The termination request details

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created termination request

functions.name: createVacancy
functions.description:
Create a vacancy in a swarm.

Creates a new vacancy that agents can apply for. Vacancies represent
open positions within teams or roles that need to be filled by
qualified agents.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The vacancy creation information

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created SwarmVacancy object

functions.name: deleteRole
functions.description:
Delete a role from a swarm.

Permanently removes a role and unassigns all agents from it.
Agents will lose the permissions and responsibilities associated
with the role.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the role to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the role is deleted

functions.name: deleteSwarm
functions.description:
Delete a swarm by ID.

Permanently removes a swarm and all its associated resources including
teams, roles, agents, and configurations. This action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the swarm is deleted

functions.name: deleteTeam
functions.description:
Delete a team from a swarm.

Permanently removes a team and all its associated memberships.
Agents will be unassigned from the team but remain in the swarm.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the team to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the team is deleted

functions.name: getAgentsByStatus
functions.description:
Get agents filtered by status in a swarm.

Retrieves all agents in a swarm that match a specific status, such as
all available agents or all agents in error state. This is useful for
finding agents ready for task assignment.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The status to filter by (available, busy, offline, etc.)

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of matching SwarmAgentInfo objects

functions.name: getAgentStatus
functions.description:
Get the status of a specific agent in a swarm.

Retrieves current status information for an agent including its
state, last activity timestamp, and any status messages. Use this
for monitoring agent availability and health.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the SwarmAgentInfo object with status details

functions.name: getConfig
functions.description:
Get the configuration of a swarm.

Retrieves the current configuration settings for a swarm, including
operational parameters, limits, and behavioral settings. Use this
to view swarm settings or for configuration management.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the SwarmConfig object

functions.name: getExecutionStatus
functions.description:
Get the execution status of a swarm.

Retrieves real-time information about swarm execution including
current tasks, agent activity, progress metrics, and any errors
or warnings encountered.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the SwarmStatusUpdate object with execution details

functions.name: getLayout
functions.description:
Get the swarm layout.

Retrieves the current layout configuration for swarms in the system.
The layout defines how swarms, teams, and agents are organized and
displayed in user interfaces.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the SwarmLayout object

functions.name: getRole
functions.description:
Get a role by ID within a swarm.

Retrieves detailed information about a specific role including its
permissions and assigned agents. Use this for displaying role details
or managing role assignments.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the role

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the SwarmRole object

functions.name: getRoleAgents
functions.description:
Get agents assigned to a specific role.

Retrieves all agents that currently have the specified role assigned
to them. Use this to view role membership or manage role assignments.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the role

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmAgentInfo objects with the role

functions.name: getRoleStatistics
functions.description:
Get role statistics for a swarm.

Retrieves statistical information about all roles in a swarm,
including assignment counts, utilization rates, and activity metrics.
Use this for monitoring role distribution and workload balance.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmRoleStatistics objects

functions.name: getSpawnRegistry
functions.description:
Get the spawn registry for a swarm.

Retrieves the spawn registry containing information about all
agents that have been spawned for the swarm, including their
configuration and lifecycle status.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the spawn registry data

functions.name: getSwarm
functions.description:
Get a swarm by ID.

Retrieves detailed information about a specific swarm including
its configuration, teams, roles, and agents. Use this for displaying
swarm details or managing swarm state.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the SwarmData object

functions.name: getSwarmStatus
functions.description:
Get the overall status of a swarm.

Retrieves comprehensive status information about a swarm including
its operational state, agent availability, team health, and overall
readiness to execute tasks.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the SwarmStatusUpdate object

functions.name: getTeam
functions.description:
Get a team by ID within a swarm.

Retrieves detailed information about a specific team including its
members, roles, and configuration. Use this for displaying team
details or managing team state.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the team

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the SwarmTeam object

functions.name: getTeamMembers
functions.description:
Get the members of a team within a swarm.

Retrieves all agents that are currently members of the specified team.
Use this to view team composition or manage team membership.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the team

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmAgentInfo objects representing team members

functions.name: getTeamStatistics
functions.description:
Get team statistics for a swarm.

Retrieves statistical information about all teams in a swarm,
including member counts, activity levels, and performance metrics.
Use this for monitoring team health and utilization.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmTeamStatistics objects

functions.name: getTerminationRegistry
functions.description:
Get the termination registry for a swarm.

Retrieves the termination registry containing information about
all agents that have been terminated from the swarm, including
their lifecycle history and termination reasons.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the termination registry data

functions.name: healthCheck
functions.description:
Health check for swarm service.

Returns service status, useful for monitoring and health dashboards.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to service status

functions.name: joinTeam
functions.description:
Join a team within a swarm.

Adds an agent to a team as a member. The agent will be associated
with the team and can participate in team-specific tasks and
activities.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the team to join

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional join request information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the agent has joined the team

functions.name: leaveTeam
functions.description:
Leave a team within a swarm.

Removes an agent from a team, ending their membership. The agent
will no longer participate in team activities but remains in the
swarm.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the team to leave

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional leave request information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the agent has left the team

functions.name: listAgents
functions.description:
List agents in a swarm.

Retrieves all agents registered with a specific swarm, optionally
filtered by query parameters. Use this to view available agents
or populate agent selection interfaces.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering agents

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmAgentInfo objects

functions.name: listRoles
functions.description:
List roles in a swarm.

Retrieves all roles defined in a swarm, optionally filtered by query
parameters. Use this to view available roles or populate role
selection interfaces.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering roles

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmRole objects

functions.name: listSpawnRequests
functions.description:
List spawn requests for a swarm.

Retrieves all spawn requests associated with a swarm, including
pending, approved, and completed requests. Use this to track
spawn request status and history.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering requests

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SpawnRequest objects

functions.name: listSwarms
functions.description:
List all swarms.

Retrieves all swarms in the system, optionally filtered by query
parameters. Use this to discover available swarms or populate
swarm selection interfaces.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering swarms

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmSummary objects

functions.name: listTeams
functions.description:
List teams in a swarm.

Retrieves all teams within a swarm, optionally filtered by query
parameters. Use this to view team structure or populate team
selection interfaces.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering teams

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmTeam objects

functions.name: listTerminationRequests
functions.description:
List termination requests for a swarm.

Retrieves all termination requests associated with a swarm, including
pending, approved, and completed requests. Use this to track
termination request status and history.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering requests

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of termination requests

functions.name: listVacancies
functions.description:
List vacancies in a swarm.

Retrieves all open vacancies in a swarm, optionally filtered by query
parameters. Use this to find available positions for agents to apply.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering vacancies

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of SwarmVacancy objects

functions.name: rejectSpawnRequest
functions.description:
Reject a spawn request.

Rejects a pending spawn request, preventing the creation of new
agent instances. The request will be marked as rejected and
no agents will be spawned.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the spawn request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional rejection information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the request is rejected

functions.name: rejectTerminationRequest
functions.description:
Reject a termination request.

Rejects a pending termination request, preventing the removal of
agent instances. The agents will continue operating in the swarm.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the termination request

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional rejection information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the request is rejected

functions.name: removeAgent
functions.description:
Remove an agent from a swarm.

Unregisters an agent from the swarm, removing it from all teams
and roles. The agent will no longer participate in swarm activities.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent to remove

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the agent is removed

functions.name: saveLayout
functions.description:
Save or update the swarm layout.

Persists a new layout configuration for swarms. The layout defines
how swarms, teams, and agents are organized and displayed. Use
this to save custom arrangements or update the structure.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The layout configuration to save

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the layout is saved

functions.name: startSwarm
functions.description:
Start a swarm.

Activates a swarm, enabling it to begin processing tasks and
coordinating agent activities. The swarm will start executing
its assigned workflow according to its configuration.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional start parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the swarm has started

functions.name: stopSwarm
functions.description:
Stop a swarm.

Deactivates a swarm, halting all in-progress tasks and agent
activities. The swarm will gracefully shut down and return to
an idle state.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional stop parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the swarm has stopped

functions.name: unassignRole
functions.description:
Unassign a role from an agent in a swarm.

Removes a role from an agent, revoking the associated permissions
and responsibilities. The agent will no longer be able to perform
actions authorized by this role.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the role to unassign

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The role unassignment information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the role is unassigned

functions.name: updateAgentStatus
functions.description:
Update the status of an agent in a swarm.

Changes the operational status of an agent, such as marking it as
available, busy, offline, or in error. Status updates affect agent
assignment and task distribution.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the agent

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The status update information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the status is updated

functions.name: updateConfig
functions.description:
Update the configuration of a swarm.

Modifies the configuration settings for a swarm. Changes take effect
immediately and affect all subsequent swarm operations. Use this
to adjust swarm behavior and operational parameters.

parameters.name: swarmId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the swarm

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The configuration updates

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the configuration is updated

functions.name: checkCli
functions.description:
Checks the current CLI installation status.

Returns information about whether the CLI tool is installed, which
version is present, and if it's properly configured. Use this to
verify CLI availability before attempting to use it.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to CLI status information

functions.name: installCli
functions.description:
Installs the CodeBolt CLI tool on the system.

Downloads and configures the command-line interface tool for CodeBolt,
enabling terminal-based operations and command execution. The installation
process includes setting up necessary binaries and configuration files.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional parameters for CLI installation

returns.description: A promise that resolves to installation status and details

functions.name: openFolder
functions.description:
Opens a folder in the system's native file manager.

Launches the default file explorer or Finder window for the specified
folder path. This provides convenient access to project directories
and workspace locations directly from the application.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Request containing folder information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the folder has been opened

functions.name: bulkCreate
functions.description: Bulk create activities

functions.name: create
functions.description: Create a new task activity

functions.name: createPrestart
functions.description: Create a prestart activity

functions.name: delete
functions.description: Delete a task activity

functions.name: deleteOlder
functions.description: Delete older activities (cleanup)

functions.name: get
functions.description: Get a specific task activity

functions.name: getByStatus
functions.description: Get activities by status

functions.name: getByThread
functions.description: Get activities by thread ID

functions.name: getByType
functions.description: Get activities by type

functions.name: getDetailsWithActivities
functions.description: Get task details with activities

functions.name: getRecent
functions.description: Get recent activities

functions.name: getStatistics
functions.description: Get activity statistics overview

functions.name: list
functions.description: List all task activities

functions.name: update
functions.description: Update a task activity

functions.name: addChild
functions.description:
Adds a child task to an existing parent task.

Creates a new task as a child of the specified parent task, establishing
a hierarchical relationship. Child tasks represent subtasks or components
that contribute to completing the parent task. Returns the new child task.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent task

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The child task creation request

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created child Task object

functions.name: addMessage
functions.description:
Adds a message to a task's message thread.

Appends a new message to the task's communication thread. Messages
can be used for notes, comments, status updates, or other
communication related to the task. Returns the created message.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to add the message to

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The message creation request

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created TaskMessage object

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new task in the system.

Creates a new task with the provided configuration. The task can be
a standalone unit of work or can later have child tasks added to create
a task hierarchy. Returns the created task with its generated ID.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The task creation request containing task properties

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created Task object

functions.name: delete
functions.description:
Permanently deletes a task from the system.

Removes the specified task and all its associated data. If the task
has child tasks, they may be affected depending on system configuration.
This operation cannot be undone.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the task has been deleted

functions.name: deleteCompletedTasks
functions.description:
Deletes all completed tasks across all projects.

Removes every task with 'completed' status in the entire system.
This is a global cleanup operation that affects all projects.
Use with caution as this operation cannot be undone.

returns.description: A promise that resolves when all completed tasks have been deleted

functions.name: deleteProjectCompletedTasks
functions.description:
Deletes all completed tasks for a specific project.

Removes all tasks with 'completed' status that belong to the specified
project. Use this for cleanup operations to maintain a lean task
history. This operation cannot be undone.

parameters.name: projectPath
parameters.description: The filesystem path identifying the target project

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the completed tasks have been deleted

functions.name: get
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific task by its unique identifier.

Returns the complete task object including all properties, metadata,
and relationships. Use this to get full task details after a search or
when you need the complete task representation.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the Task object with the specified ID

functions.name: getChildren
functions.description:
Retrieves all child tasks of a parent task.

Returns all tasks that have been added as children of the specified
parent task. This provides access to the task's subtasks and allows
for navigation of the task hierarchy.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent task

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of child Task objects

functions.name: getForCurrentProject
functions.description:
Retrieves all tasks associated with the current project.

Returns tasks that belong to the currently active project context.
This is useful for project-specific task views and when you need to
operate on tasks within the current working project.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of Task objects for the current project

functions.name: getForProject
functions.description:
Retrieves all tasks for a specific project path.

Returns tasks that belong to the project identified by its filesystem
path. This allows accessing tasks from any project regardless of the
current working directory context.

parameters.name: projectPath
parameters.description: The filesystem path identifying the target project

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of Task objects for the specified project

functions.name: getHierarchy
functions.description:
Retrieves the complete hierarchy for a task.

Returns a hierarchical tree representation starting from the specified
task, including all descendants and their relationships. This provides
a complete view of the task's entire subtree for visualization or
comprehensive analysis.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the root task

returns.description: A promise that resolves to a TaskHierarchy object containing the full tree structure

functions.name: getProjectStatistics
functions.description:
Retrieves statistics for tasks in the current project.

Returns aggregated statistics specific to the current project context,
including task counts by status, priority distribution, and other
project-specific metrics. Ideal for project dashboards.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to TaskStatistics for the current project

functions.name: getStatistics
functions.description:
Retrieves overall task statistics across all projects.

Returns aggregated statistics including total task counts, status
distribution, and other metrics. Use this for dashboard displays or
high-level reporting on task activity across the entire platform.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to TaskStatistics containing overview metrics

functions.name: healthCheck
functions.description:
Performs a health check on the task service.

Verifies that the task management service is operational and responding.
Use this to monitor service availability or for health monitoring in
production environments.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an object containing the service status

functions.name: markCompleted
functions.description:
Marks a task as completed.

Updates the task's status to indicate that all work has been finished.
This is typically called when a task's objectives have been fully
achieved. Returns the updated task object.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to mark as completed

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated Task object with completed status

functions.name: markFailed
functions.description:
Marks a task as failed.

Updates the task's status to indicate that the task could not be
completed successfully. This is typically called when errors occur or
when task objectives cannot be achieved. Returns the updated task object.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to mark as failed

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated Task object with failed status

functions.name: markInProgress
functions.description:
Marks a task as in-progress.

Updates the task's status to indicate that work has begun on it.
This is typically called when a task is being actively worked on.
Returns the updated task object.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to mark as in-progress

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated Task object with in-progress status

functions.name: search
functions.description:
Searches for tasks using optional query parameters.

Returns tasks that match the specified search criteria. Use this method
to find tasks by various attributes including status, priority, date
ranges, or custom filters. Returns an empty array if no tasks match.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional search parameters for filtering and sorting tasks

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of matching Task objects

functions.name: update
functions.description:
Updates an existing task with new information.

Modifies the specified task with the provided updates. Only the
fields included in the request will be modified; other fields remain
unchanged. Returns the updated task object.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The update request containing fields to modify

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated Task object

functions.name: getAll
functions.description:
Retrieves all available templates.

Returns every template in the system regardless of type or category.
Use this for comprehensive template browsing or to populate a full
template gallery.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of Template objects

functions.name: list
functions.description:
Lists templates with optional filtering.

Returns templates matching the provided query parameters. This is
useful for finding templates by type, category, or other criteria
without loading the entire template catalog.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering templates

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of matching Template objects

functions.name: getActiveProcesses
functions.description:
Retrieves all currently active terminal processes.

Returns terminal processes that are currently running, including
their process IDs, commands, and status information. Use this to
monitor what shell operations are in progress.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of active TerminalProcess objects

functions.name: stopAllProcesses
functions.description:
Stops all running terminal processes.

Terminates every currently active terminal process in the system.
Use this for cleanup operations or when shutting down the platform
to ensure no orphaned processes remain.

returns.description: A promise that resolves when all processes have been stopped

functions.name: stopProcess
functions.description:
Stops a specific terminal process by its ID.

Sends a termination signal to the specified terminal process,
ending its execution gracefully if possible. The process will be
removed from the active process list.

parameters.name: processId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the terminal process to stop

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the process has been stopped

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new custom theme.

Registers a new theme with the system using the provided configuration.
The theme can be customized with specific colors, fonts, and styling
preferences to match design requirements.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Request containing the new theme configuration

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created Theme object

functions.name: delete
functions.description:
Deletes a theme from the system.

Permanently removes the specified theme. If the theme is currently
active, another theme will need to be set as active. This operation
cannot be undone.

parameters.name: themeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the theme to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the theme has been deleted

functions.name: getAll
functions.description:
Retrieves all available themes in the system.

Returns every theme regardless of whether it's active or inactive.
Use this for comprehensive theme browsing or to populate theme selection UIs.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of Theme objects

functions.name: getById
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific theme by its unique identifier.

Returns detailed information about a single theme including its
configuration, colors, settings, and metadata.

parameters.name: themeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the theme to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the requested Theme object

functions.name: getStats
functions.description:
Retrieves theme statistics and metrics.

Returns aggregated data about themes including counts, usage statistics,
and other analytical information useful for monitoring and reporting.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to a ThemeStats object containing metrics

functions.name: reset
functions.description:
Resets themes to their default configuration.

Restores all theme settings to the original system defaults.
Optionally accepts custom data to control the reset behavior.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional parameters for controlling the reset operation

returns.description: A promise that resolves when themes have been reset

functions.name: seedPopular
functions.description:
Seeds the system with popular theme presets.

Creates a collection of well-known and widely-used themes based on
popular color schemes and design systems. Useful for initializing
the theme catalog with common options.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional parameters to control the seeding process

returns.description: A promise that resolves when popular themes have been seeded

functions.name: update
functions.description:
Updates an existing theme's configuration.

Modifies the properties, colors, or settings of a theme identified
by its ID. Use this to make incremental changes to a theme without
recreating it from scratch.

parameters.name: themeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the theme to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Request containing the theme updates to apply

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated Theme object

functions.name: updateColors
functions.description:
Updates the color scheme of a theme.

Modifies the color palette for one or more themes. This includes
primary colors, background colors, text colors, and other color
variables used throughout the interface.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Request containing theme color update information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when colors have been updated

functions.name: validate
functions.description:
Validates theme data for correctness and completeness.

Checks the current theme configuration for errors, inconsistencies,
or missing required fields. Returns detailed validation results
highlighting any issues found.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to a ThemeValidationResult with validation status

functions.name: advancedSearch
functions.description:
Performs an advanced search for threads based on multiple criteria.

Allows complex queries with filtering, sorting, and pagination to find
threads matching specific conditions. Use this for flexible thread discovery
and management operations.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The search request containing filter and query parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of matching Thread objects

functions.name: autoUpdateName
functions.description:
Automatically updates a thread's name based on its content.

Analyzes the thread's execution history, messages, and context to generate
an appropriate name that reflects its purpose and activity. This is useful
for maintaining descriptive thread labels without manual intervention.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The request containing the thread ID to rename

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread name has been updated

functions.name: bulkDelete
functions.description:
Deletes multiple threads in a single operation.

Removes the specified threads from the system in a bulk operation.
This is more efficient than deleting threads individually and ensures
atomic deletion of multiple threads.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The bulk delete request containing thread IDs to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when all threads have been deleted

functions.name: bulkUpdate
functions.description:
Updates multiple threads in a single operation.

Performs bulk modifications across multiple threads simultaneously.
This is more efficient than updating threads individually when you need
to apply the same changes to many threads.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The bulk update request containing thread IDs and updates

returns.description: A promise that resolves when all threads have been updated

functions.name: cancel
functions.description:
Cancels the execution of a thread.

Terminates the thread's operations and places it in a cancelled state.
Unlike pausing, this action cannot be reversed and the thread will not
continue execution. Use this to stop threads that are no longer needed.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to cancel

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been cancelled

functions.name: complete
functions.description:
Marks a thread as completed.

Sets the thread's status to completed, indicating that its execution
has finished successfully. This is typically done automatically by
the thread, but can be called manually when needed.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to complete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been marked complete

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new thread with the specified configuration.

Initializes a new thread with the provided settings and parameters.
The thread will be created in an initial state and can be executed
when ready. Use this to set up new execution units.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The thread creation request with configuration details

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the newly created Thread object

functions.name: delete
functions.description:
Deletes a thread from the system.

Permanently removes the specified thread and all its associated data.
This action cannot be undone, so use with caution. Deleting a thread
will also remove its relationships with other threads.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been deleted

functions.name: execute
functions.description:
Executes a thread with optional configuration parameters.

Starts or resumes execution of the specified thread. You can provide
additional execution parameters to customize the run. The thread will
process its configured tasks and operations.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to execute

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional execution configuration parameters

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated Thread object

functions.name: getById
functions.description:
Retrieves a thread by its unique identifier.

Fetches the complete thread object including all its properties and
current state. This is the primary method for accessing thread details
when you know the thread's ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the Thread object

functions.name: getChildren
functions.description:
Retrieves all direct child threads of a specified thread.

Returns threads that are immediate descendants of the given parent thread.
This is useful for navigating thread hierarchies and managing related threads.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent thread

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of child Thread objects

functions.name: getDependencies
functions.description:
Retrieves all threads that the specified thread depends on.

Returns threads that are prerequisites or dependencies of the given thread.
This is useful for understanding execution order requirements and managing
complex thread relationships.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of dependent Thread objects

functions.name: getGraph
functions.description:
Retrieves the complete thread graph structure.

Returns all threads as nodes in a graph representation, showing relationships
and connections between threads. This is useful for visualizing thread
hierarchies and dependencies.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of ThreadGraphNode objects

functions.name: getStatistics
functions.description:
Retrieves statistics about threads in the system.

Returns aggregated metrics including total thread counts, status distributions,
and other statistical data. Use this to gain insights into thread activity
and system performance.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to ThreadStatistics containing various metrics

functions.name: getThreadInfo
functions.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific thread.

Returns comprehensive metadata and status information for the specified
thread, including its configuration, current state, and related attributes.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to ThreadInfo containing thread details

functions.name: getTimeline
functions.description:
Retrieves the timeline of events for a specific thread.

Returns a chronological list of all events associated with the thread,
including status changes, executions, and other significant occurrences.
Use this to track thread activity and audit execution history.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of ThreadTimelineEvent objects

functions.name: getTree
functions.description:
Retrieves the hierarchical tree structure for a thread.

Returns the complete tree representation starting from the specified thread,
including all descendant threads and their relationships. This is useful for
visualizing thread hierarchies and understanding nested thread structures.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the root thread

returns.description: A promise that resolves to a ThreadGraphNode representing the tree

functions.name: healthCheck
functions.description:
Performs a health check on the thread service.

Verifies that the thread service is operational and responding to requests.
Use this for service monitoring or to check availability before performing
other thread operations.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an object containing the service status

functions.name: list
functions.description:
Lists all threads with optional filtering.

Returns threads matching the provided query parameters, allowing you to
filter and paginate through the thread collection. Use this to browse
threads or find specific sets of threads based on criteria.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering threads

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of Thread objects

functions.name: pause
functions.description:
Pauses the execution of a running thread.

Suspends the thread's current operations and places it in a paused state.
The thread can be resumed later using the resume method. Use this to
temporarily halt execution without cancelling the thread.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to pause

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been paused

functions.name: resume
functions.description:
Resumes execution of a paused thread.

Reactivates a thread that was previously paused, allowing it to continue
its operations from where it left off. The thread will return to an
active state and continue processing.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to resume

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been resumed

functions.name: searchThread
functions.description:
Searches for a specific thread by its ID.

Performs a direct lookup to find a thread using its unique identifier.
This is the most efficient way to retrieve a thread when you know its ID.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to search for

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the matching Thread object

functions.name: update
functions.description:
Updates an existing thread with new information.

Modifies the specified thread's configuration, metadata, or other
attributes. Only the fields provided in the request will be updated.
Use this to make changes to existing threads.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The update request containing the fields to modify

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated Thread object

functions.name: updateProgress
functions.description:
Updates the progress information for a thread.

Modifies the thread's progress metrics, such as completion percentage,
current step, or other progress indicators. This is useful for tracking
execution progress and providing feedback to users.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The request containing progress update information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the progress has been updated

functions.name: updateStatus
functions.description:
Updates the status of a specific thread.

Changes the execution state of the thread to a new status such as active,
paused, or completed. This controls the thread's lifecycle and availability
for execution.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The request containing the new status information

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the status has been updated

functions.name: archiveThread
functions.description:
Archives a todo thread.

Marks the specified thread as archived, removing it from active view
while preserving all todos within it. Archived threads can still be
accessed but are typically hidden from default views.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to archive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been archived

functions.name: bulkCreate
functions.description:
Bulk-creates multiple todos at once.

Creates multiple todo items in a single API call, which is more efficient
than creating them individually. Use this for batch operations, importing
tasks, or initializing a thread with multiple items.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Bulk todo creation payload containing multiple items

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of created TodoItem objects

functions.name: create
functions.description:
Creates a new todo.

Initializes a new todo item with the provided data. The todo can be
assigned to a thread, given a priority, and include any other relevant
task information.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Todo creation payload containing task details

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created TodoItem object

functions.name: createThread
functions.description:
Creates a new todo thread.

Initializes a new thread for organizing related todos. Threads
provide structure and context for groups of tasks, making them
easier to manage and track together.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Thread creation payload containing name and optional metadata

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the created TodoList thread object

functions.name: delete
functions.description:
Deletes a todo by ID.

Permanently removes the specified todo from the system. This operation
cannot be undone. Use with caution and consider alternatives like
archiving if the todo should be preserved for historical purposes.

parameters.name: todoId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the todo to delete

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the todo has been deleted

functions.name: deleteAllCompleted
functions.description:
Deletes all completed todos, optionally filtered by thread.

Removes all todos with a completed status. When a threadId is provided,
only completed todos within that specific thread are deleted. This is
useful for cleanup operations to maintain a tidy task list.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering deletion scope

returns.description: A promise that resolves when all completed todos have been deleted

functions.name: exportTodos
functions.description:
Exports todos, optionally filtered by thread.

Returns todo data in a format suitable for external systems or backup.
The export can be scoped to a specific thread or include all todos
in the system. The exact format depends on server configuration.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering the export

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the exported todo data

functions.name: get
functions.description:
Retrieves a specific todo by ID.

Returns detailed information about a single todo item including
its current status, description, assignee, and all other fields.
Use this to get the full details of a particular task.

parameters.name: todoId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the todo to retrieve

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the requested TodoItem object

functions.name: getAll
functions.description:
Retrieves all todos, optionally filtered by thread.

Returns all todo items in the system. When a threadId is provided,
only todos belonging to that specific thread are returned. This
is useful for displaying task lists scoped to a particular context.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering todos

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of TodoItem objects

functions.name: getStats
functions.description:
Retrieves todo statistics, optionally filtered by thread.

Returns aggregated statistics about todos including counts by status,
priority distribution, and completion rates. When a threadId is provided,
statistics are scoped to that specific thread only.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional query parameters for filtering statistics

returns.description: A promise that resolves to TodoStats object with metrics

functions.name: getThreads
functions.description:
Retrieves all todo threads.

Returns all thread objects that organize todos into logical groups.
Threads can represent projects, features, or any other categorization
scheme. Use this to get an overview of all todo collections.

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of TodoList thread objects

functions.name: importTodos
functions.description:
Imports todos from an external source.

Imports todo items from an external system or file format. This enables
migrating tasks from other todo management tools or bulk loading
tasks from predefined lists.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Import payload containing external todo data

returns.description: A promise that resolves to an array of imported TodoItem objects

functions.name: unarchiveThread
functions.description:
Unarchives a todo thread.

Removes the archived status from the specified thread, making it
visible in active views again. This restores the thread and all its
todos to normal operational status.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the thread to unarchive

returns.description: A promise that resolves when the thread has been unarchived

functions.name: update
functions.description:
Updates an existing todo.

Modifies the specified todo with the provided field values. Only the
fields included in the request will be updated; all other fields remain
unchanged. Use this to change status, reassign tasks, or update details.

parameters.name: todoId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the todo to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Partial update payload containing fields to modify

returns.description: A promise that resolves to the updated TodoItem object
